data.title: Accenture Monitors AI Tool Usage Among Employees
data.description:
Accenture is closely observing the use of AI tools by its employees, with a particular focus on senior staff. According to NS3.AI, the company has made regular AI adoption a key performance indicator for promotions. Accenture has trained the majority of its workforce in generative AI and is investing significantly in AI learning programs. The company has indicated that employees who resist adopting AI may face termination.

Broader surveys reveal an increase in AI usage, yet there is a decline in worker confidence. Older employees, in particular, are experiencing anxiety over potential job shifts induced by AI.

data.title: Mexico Halts Oil Shipments to Cuba Amid Political Tensions
data.description: Mexico has decided to stop sending oil to Cuba, a move that carries significant political and historical implications. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this decision by Claudia Sheinbaum is not solely about energy concerns but also reflects deeper political motivations. The suspension of oil shipments marks a shift in Mexico's foreign policy approach towards Cuba, potentially impacting the longstanding relationship between the two nations. This development comes amid broader geopolitical dynamics in the region, as countries navigate complex political landscapes.

data.title: Walgreens Announces Nationwide Employee Layoffs Amid Cost-Cutting Measures
data.description: Walgreens is set to lay off hundreds of employees across the United States as part of a cost-cutting initiative by its new private equity owners. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the pharmacy chain's efforts to streamline operations amid financial challenges. The layoffs come as Walgreens seeks to optimize its business model and improve profitability in a competitive market. The company has not disclosed specific details regarding the affected positions or the timeline for the layoffs. Walgreens' decision reflects broader trends in the retail and healthcare sectors, where companies are increasingly focusing on efficiency and cost management to navigate economic pressures.

data.title: CME Group launches 24/7 trading for crypto futures
data.description:
CME Group launches 24/7 trading for crypto futures and options. Notional volume reached around $3 trillion in 2025. The article CME Group launches 24/7 trading for crypto futures was first published on Crypto Valley Journal.
source: https://cryptovalleyjournal.com/investing/financial-products/cme-group-launches-247-trading-for-crypto-futures/?utm_campaign=rss_reader&utm_medium=rss&utm_source=feed_source&utm_content=CME+Group+launches+24%2F7+trading+for+crypto+futures

data.title: Polymarket Acquires Dome to Enhance Prediction Market Connectivity
data.description: Polymarket has announced the acquisition of Dome, a startup specializing in API technology that facilitates connectivity across various prediction market platforms. According to NS3.AI, this acquisition is Polymarket's second, following its previous purchase of the derivatives exchange QCEX. Dome's technology is anticipated to improve Polymarket's capacity to integrate and streamline data from prediction markets.

data.title: Solana's Price Consolidation Reflects Market Uncertainty
data.description: Solana has been consolidating below $90 for nearly two weeks, indicating uncertainty in the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, on-chain indicators suggest a gradual recovery, yet selling pressure due to losses continues to limit immediate upside potential. Institutional inflows are providing some support, although technical resistance at $87 and support at $78 are defining Solana's near-term price range.

data.title: H.I.G. Capital Focuses on Hard Assets and Middle Market Strategy
data.description: H.I.G. Capital is concentrating on retaining hard assets while refining its strategy in the middle market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the firm's approach to navigating current economic conditions. Additionally, OpenAI is pursuing a significant fundraising effort, and Elliott Management is exerting pressure on the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG). These developments reflect ongoing strategic maneuvers in the financial sector as companies adapt to evolving market dynamics.

data.title: UAE’s $450M Bitcoin mining haul faces market drop — But miners aren’t selling
data.description:
On-chain data shows UAE-linked Bitcoin miners have mined over $450m worth of BTC and continue to hold supply, as miner revenue stabilizes.
source: https://ambcrypto.com/uaes-450m-bitcoin-mining-haul-faces-market-drop-but-miners-arent-selling/

data.title: SUI Drops Below $1 Despite Launch of First U.S. Staking ETFs by Grayscale and Canary
data.description:
The debut of the first U. S. -listed staking ETFs tied to SUI was expected to mark a turning point for the token. Instead, the crypto slipped below the $1 level, showing the gap between growing institutional access and weakening market sentiment
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/sui-drops-below-1-despite-launch-of-first-u-s-staking-etfs-by-grayscale-and-canary/

data.title: CME Group Expands Into 24/7 Crypto Trading in Major Shift
data.description:
CME Group will launch 24/7 cryptocurrency futures and options trading beginning May 29, marking a structural shift in how regulated crypto derivatives operate in the United States. The move will allow clients to trade Bitcoin and Ether futures contracts continuously, aligning regulated markets more closely with crypto’s always-on nature. com
source: https://techgaged.com/cme-group-expands-into-24-7-crypto-trading-in-major-shift/

data.title: Ares Doubles European Private Credit CLOs with Second Offering
data.description: Ares Management Corporation has successfully priced its second European direct lending collateralized loan obligation (CLO), marking a significant milestone in the region's private credit market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing investor interest in this emerging asset class. The move effectively doubles the total number of private credit CLOs in Europe, reflecting a warming sentiment among investors towards these financial instruments. As the market evolves, Ares continues to expand its footprint, capitalizing on the increasing demand for diversified credit products.

data.title: Trump Faces Deadline to Address Conflict of Interest in IRS Lawsuit
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has been given a deadline of next week to respond to allegations that his $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) presents a significant conflict of interest. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the legal challenge that questions the impartiality of Trump's actions in relation to the IRS. The lawsuit, which seeks substantial damages, has raised concerns about potential conflicts due to Trump's position and influence. Legal experts are closely monitoring the situation as the deadline approaches, with implications for both the presidency and the IRS's operations. The outcome of this legal matter could have far-reaching effects on the relationship between the executive branch and federal tax authorities.

data.title: Ethereum's 29% Decline Reflected in Beeple's Post-Apocalyptic Art
data.description: NFT artist Beeple has portrayed the recent ETHDenver conference as a post-apocalyptic wasteland, symbolizing Ethereum's 29% price decline over the past year. According to NS3.AI, the conference saw a significant drop in attendance and side events compared to previous years, evoking nostalgia for pre-boom times and highlighting a decrease in enthusiasm within the Ethereum community. Despite this bleak depiction, some insiders and supporters remain optimistic, focusing on technical progress and positive developments.

data.title: Limited Passage at Rafah Crossing in Gaza Falls Short of Expectations
data.description:
The Rafah crossing in southern Gaza, which reopened for limited passage earlier this month, has seen fewer crossings than anticipated. According to RTHK, Gaza authorities reported that only over 1,100 Palestinians have passed through the Rafah crossing, which is about one-third of the initially expected number.

Data released by Gaza authorities indicate that from the reopening on the 2nd of this month until the 18th, a total of 1,148 individuals crossed the border. Among them, 640 people left Gaza, while 508 returned. Additionally, 26 individuals were denied exit from Gaza for unspecified reasons.

Under the ceasefire agreement, the reopening of the Rafah crossing was expected to allow 50 Palestinians to return to Gaza daily, along with permitting 50 patients from Gaza and two accompanying individuals each to travel to Egypt.

data.title: ‘Resilient’ Bitcoin holders defend BTC, but bear floor sits 20% lower: Glassnode
data.description:
Bitcoin trades in a tight demand zone that formed in 2024, but previous bear market data suggest the channel will break and lead to new lows.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/resilient-bitcoin-holders-defend-btc-but-bear-floor-sits-20-lower-glassnode?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Blockfills Halts Client Transactions Following $75 Million Lending Loss
data.description: Blockfills, a cryptocurrency trading platform based in Chicago and backed by Susquehanna, has temporarily paused client deposits and withdrawals. According to NS3.AI, this decision comes after the company incurred a substantial $75 million loss in its lending operations due to challenging market and financial conditions. The firm is currently evaluating the situation before resuming standard operations.

data.title: Blockfills Faces Sale Amid $75 Million Loan Loss Due to Market Volatility
data.description: Crypto lending platform Blockfills is considering selling its business following a significant $75 million loan loss attributed to recent market volatility. According to NS3.AI, the company has also suspended customer deposits and withdrawals last week in response to the downturn. This situation underscores the persistent challenges faced by crypto lending firms amid unstable market conditions.
